questions.q: What is the main function of the notochord in the embryonic development of Chordata?
questions.answer: It provides mechanical support and gives the embryo its shape.

questions.q: The presence of the following feature is characteristic of Acoelomates?
Name the type of muscle tissue found in the walls of hollow organs such as the intestine.
State the significance of the presence of a mesoderm in the development of Chordata.
questions.answer: It gives rise to various bodily structures such as muscles, blood vessels, and connective tissues.

questions.q: What is the difference between the structure of the exoskeleton and the endoskeleton?
questions.answer: Exoskeleton is external, while endoskeleton is internal.

questions.q: Explain the significance of the presence of a nervous system in animals.
questions.answer: It controls and coordinates various bodily functions, helps in sensing the environment, and enables the animal to respond to stimuli.

questions.q: What is the main difference between the structure of a prokaryotic cell and an animal cell?
questions.answer: The presence of a nucleus and other membrane-bound organelles in an animal cell.

questions.q: The following feature is characteristic of Pseudocoelomates?
Compare and contrast the structure of a vertebrate and an invertebrate.
questions.answer: A vertebrate has a vertebral column, whereas an invertebrate does not. Vertebrates also have a well-developed excretory system, whereas invertebrates have a simpler system.

questions.q: What is the main function of the epithelial tissue in the body?
questions.answer: It forms the lining of various bodily structures such as glands, organs, and body surfaces.
